member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.AnimationConfig
member.summary: Per-animation capture settings that control how frames are sampled from an animation clip.

remarks.para: and are normalized values (0.0–1.0) that define a sub-range of the animation to capture. Combined with , this allows capturing only the interesting portion of an animation at a modified playback speed.

member.param: Number of frames to capture from this animation. Defaults to 8.
Playback speed override (1.0 = normal speed). Defaults to 1.0.
Normalized start time — skip the beginning of the animation (0.0–1.0). Defaults to 0.0.
Normalized end time — skip the end of the animation (0.0–1.0). Defaults to 1.0.
Loop mode for the output sprite animation metadata. Defaults to None.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.AnimationSampling
member.summary: Generates frame timestamp sequences for capturing animation frames at uniform intervals.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.AnimationSampling.GenerateUniform(System.Single,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a uniform frame sequence spanning the full animation duration. Each timestamp is placed at the center of its time window.
member.remarks: For 8 frames, produces timestamps: [0.0625, 0.1875, 0.3125, 0.4375, 0.5625, 0.6875, 0.8125, 0.9375]. Center-of-window placement avoids capturing the exact start (often a T-pose) or exact end of animations.
member.param: Total animation duration in seconds. Must be positive.
Number of frames to capture. Must be positive.
member.exception: Thrown when or is not positive.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.AnimationSampling.GenerateFromConfig(BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.AnimationInfo,BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.AnimationConfig)
member.summary: Generates a frame sequence from an animation info and capture configuration, applying trim range and speed multiplier adjustments.
member.remarks: Timestamps are distributed uniformly within the [TrimStart, TrimEnd] range. The effective duration accounts for both the trim range and speed multiplier.
member.param: Animation metadata from the engine bridge.
Per-animation capture configuration.
member.returns: A with timestamps adjusted for trim and speed.
member.exception: Thrown when is not positive, or is not greater than .

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.FrameSequence
member.summary: An ordered list of normalized frame timestamps for one animation capture, produced by .
member.remarks: Each timestamp is a normalized value (0.0–1.0) representing when within the animation duration the frame should be captured. Timestamps are placed at the center of each time window for uniform sampling.
member.param: Normalized times (0.0–1.0) for each frame to capture.
Effective animation duration in seconds (after trim and speed adjustments).
Number of frames in the sequence.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Animation.FrameSequence.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Single},System.Single,System.Int32)

member.param: Normalized times (0.0–1.0) for each frame to capture.
Effective animation duration in seconds (after trim and speed adjustments).
Number of frames in the sequence.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Atlas.AtlasPacker
member.summary: Packs variable-sized frame rectangles into one or more atlas images using the MaxRects-BSSF (Best Short Side Fit) bin-packing algorithm.

remarks.para: The algorithm maintains a list of free rectangles representing unoccupied atlas regions. For each frame, the free rectangle with the smallest short-side remainder is selected (BSSF heuristic). After placement, the free space is subdivided into up to four new rectangles, and fully-contained duplicates are pruned.
When no free rectangle can fit the current frame, a new atlas is started transparently. The result includes per-atlas dimensions (optionally rounded to power-of-two) and overall packing efficiency.
Reference: Jukka Jylänki, "A Thousand Ways to Pack the Bin" (2010).

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Atlas.AtlasPacker.Pack(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32}},BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Atlas.AtlasOptions)
member.summary: Packs a list of frames into one or more atlas images using MaxRects-BSSF.
member.param: Input frames to pack. Each tuple contains the frame's width, height, and original index.
Atlas packing configuration (max size, padding, power-of-two, etc.).
member.returns: An containing per-frame placements and per-atlas dimensions.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Atlas.AtlasPacker.FindBestRect(System.Collections.Generic.List{BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Rectangle},System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Finds the free rectangle with the Best Short Side Fit for the given padded dimensions.

member.summary: Removes any free rectangle that is fully contained within another free rectangle.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Camera.CameraRig
member.summary: Defines how a character is captured from one orientation. Contains the projection mode, list of capture angles, frame dimensions, and rendering options.

remarks.para: Every angle in is rendered by the bridge. Mirror targets (like "NW" derived from "NE") are NOT in this list — they are computed by MirrorOptimizer from metadata.
CameraRig is immutable (C# record). Changes produce new instances, supporting undo/redo in the composer via before/after rig snapshots.

member.param: Rig identifier (e.g., "SideView-Brawler", "TopDown-8Dir").
Camera projection mode. Defaults to Orthographic.
Angles to render — every angle IS captured.
Per-frame pixel dimensions (Width, Height).
Pixels between frames in atlas. Defaults to 2.
Render clear color. Defaults to transparent.
Whether to also generate a depth-to-normal atlas.
Whether to trim transparent borders per frame.

member.summary: Creates a side-view brawler rig with one capture angle (right) that produces a horizontal mirror (left). Result: 1 rendered angle, 1 mirror = 2 total directions.

Per-frame height in pixels. Defaults to 128.

member.summary: Creates a top-down 8-direction rig with 5 capture angles (N, NE, E, SE, S) where NE, E, and SE produce horizontal mirrors (NW, W, SW). Result: 5 rendered angles, 3 mirrors = 8 total directions.
member.param: Pitch angle in degrees (negative = looking down). Defaults to -55.
Per-frame width in pixels. Defaults to 96.
Per-frame height in pixels. Defaults to 96.
member.returns: A camera rig configured for top-down 8-directional sprite capture.

member.summary: Creates a top-down 4-direction rig with 3 capture angles (N, E, S) where E produces a horizontal mirror (W). Result: 3 rendered angles, 1 mirror = 4 total directions.

Per-frame width in pixels. Defaults to 96.
Per-frame height in pixels. Defaults to 96.
member.returns: A camera rig configured for top-down 4-directional sprite capture.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Camera.CaptureAngle
member.summary: Defines a single capture angle for sprite rendering. Every CaptureAngle in a is rendered by the bridge — this is never a skip flag.

remarks.para: is additive: when true, MirrorOptimizer generates a flipped counterpart in addition to the captured frame. The angle itself is always captured regardless of this flag.
Yaw is measured in degrees around the Y axis (0 = north/forward). Pitch is measured in degrees from horizontal (0 = level, negative = looking down).

member.param: Angle identifier (e.g., "right", "N", "NE").
Degrees rotation around the Y axis (0 = north/forward).
Degrees from horizontal (0 = level, negative = looking down).
If true, MirrorOptimizer generates a horizontally flipped counterpart.
Name for the generated mirror angle (e.g., "NW"). Null if no mirror.
Flip axis for the generated mirror. Defaults to Horizontal.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Camera.OrthographicSetup
member.summary: Computes orthographic camera parameters to frame a 3D bounding box from a given capture angle with no clipping or perspective foreshortening.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Camera.OrthographicSetup.Compute(BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Camera.CaptureAngle,BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.BoundingBox,System.ValueTuple{System.Int32,System.Int32})
member.summary: Computes the orthographic camera parameters that completely frame the given bounding box when viewed from the specified capture angle.

remarks.para: The algorithm performs spherical-to-Cartesian conversion for the camera direction, positions the camera at 2.5× the bounding box half-diagonal to ensure no clipping, handles gimbal lock for near-vertical pitch angles, builds an orthonormal basis, projects all 8 bounding box corners onto the camera plane, applies a 10% safety margin, and adjusts for the frame aspect ratio.

member.param: The capture angle defining yaw and pitch.
The axis-aligned bounding box of the model.
The output frame dimensions in pixels (Width, Height).
member.returns: Complete orthographic camera parameters for engine configuration.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.ProjectionType
member.summary: Camera projection mode for sprite capture.

member.name: F:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.ProjectionType.Orthographic
member.summary: Orthographic (parallel) projection — no perspective foreshortening. Standard for sprite capture; preserves consistent sizing across depth.

member.name: F:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.ProjectionType.Perspective
member.summary: Perspective projection — objects closer to the camera appear larger. Rarely used for sprite capture; may cause inconsistent frame sizing.

member.summary: Animation playback loop behavior for sprite animations.

member.summary: Play once and stop on the last frame.

member.name: F:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.LoopMode.Loop
member.summary: Loop from the beginning after reaching the last frame.

member.name: F:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.LoopMode.PingPong

member.name: F:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.MirrorAxis.Vertical

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Export.AtlasAssembler
member.summary: Composites captured frame pixel data into atlas RGBA byte arrays at positions determined by . Each atlas is a flat RGBA byte array (4 bytes per pixel, row-major) ready for PNG encoding by the consumer.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Export.AtlasAssembler.Assemble(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Metadata.FrameCapture},BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Atlas.AtlasLayout,BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Color)
member.summary: Assembles captured frame pixel data into one or more atlas byte arrays using the placement positions from an .

remarks.para: Each atlas is initialized with the specified background color, then frame pixels are blitted in row-by-row using for efficiency.
Frame captures are matched to placements by via dictionary lookup, supporting any ordering of input frames.

member.param: Captured frame pixel data from the engine bridge.
Atlas layout computed by .
Background color to fill each atlas with before blitting frames.
member.returns: Array of atlas byte arrays, one per atlas image. Each is RGBA (4 bytes/pixel, row-major).

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Export.SpriteSheetSerializer
member.summary: Serializes and deserializes metadata to and from JSON. Uses System.Text.Json with camelCase naming, indented output, and null-value omission.

remarks.para: This is a standalone SDK — it uses System.Text.Json directly, not BannouJson, because sprite-theory has no dependency on bannou-service.
The JSON format is the canonical output for sprite sheet metadata, consumed by game runtimes in any language. Property names use camelCase to match JavaScript conventions.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Metadata.CaptureManifest
member.summary: Pre-capture estimation of expected frame counts, mirror counts, and estimated capture time. Computed from a character variant, one or more camera rigs, and a list of animations with configs.

remarks.para: Use to generate a manifest before starting a capture session. This allows progress tracking, resource estimation, and verification of expected output.
Estimated capture time assumes ~50ms per captured frame (render + readback). Mirror frames are generated from captured data and do not require additional render passes.

member.param: The character variant that will be captured.
Per-rig breakdown of expected frame counts.
Total frames requiring render passes across all rigs.
Total mirror frames generated from captured frames across all rigs.
Sum of captured and mirror frames.
Estimated capture time in milliseconds (~50ms per captured frame).
Number of animations to capture.

member.name: P:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Metadata.CaptureManifest.AnimationCount
member.summary: Number of animations to capture.
Computes a capture manifest from a character variant, camera rigs, and animation configurations.

remarks.para: For each rig, every angle in is captured (rendered). Angles with set to true additionally generate mirror frames without requiring extra render passes.

Verification example (TopDown8Dir + SideViewBrawler, 20 animations x 8 frames): TopDown8Dir has 5 angles (3 produce mirrors), SideViewBrawler has 1 angle (1 produces mirror). Captured = (5 * 160) + (1 * 160) = 960, Mirror = (3 * 160) + (1 * 160) = 640, Total = 1600.

member.param: The character variant to capture.
Camera rigs defining capture angles and frame dimensions.
Animation info and config pairs defining what to capture.
member.returns: A manifest with per-rig breakdowns and totals.

remarks.para: contains RGBA pixels (4 bytes per pixel, row-major top-to-bottom).

, when present, contains depth values normalized to 0.0 (near plane) through 1.0 (far plane). The bridge is responsible for normalizing GPU depth to this range, as different graphics APIs store depth differently. Null if depth was not captured.

Frame height in pixels.
Which CaptureAngle this frame was rendered from.
Which animation was playing during capture.
Frame number within the animation (0-based).
Animation time when captured (0.0–1.0).

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Metadata.SpriteAnimation
member.summary: Groups frames by animation name with per-angle frame index lookup for game runtime use.

remarks.para: is the primary runtime lookup. To play an animation facing a specific direction, read AngleFrameMap[angleName] to get an ordered array of frame indices, then look up each by index for atlas rect, duration, and mirror info. Mirror angles (e.g., "NW") are included in the map — they point to mirror SpriteFrame entries.

member.param: Animation clip name (matches source animation name).
Playback loop behavior (None, Loop, or PingPong).
Total animation duration in seconds.
Angle name to ordered frame index array. Includes both captured and mirror angles.
Optional per-frame event markers (hit frames, sound cues). Null if no events.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Metadata.SpriteFrame
member.summary: Per-frame metadata within a sprite sheet. Describes a single frame's position in an atlas, its animation context, pivot point, and whether it is a mirror of another frame.

remarks.para: Captured frames come first (indices 0 through N-1), mirror frames are appended after (indices N through N+M-1). This ordering is deterministic and stable.
Mirror frames share their source frame's atlas — no duplicate pixels exist in the atlas. The game engine applies a horizontal or vertical flip at render time using the flag and .

member.param: Global frame index (0-based, unique across all frames in the sprite sheet).
Which atlas image this frame resides in (0 for single-atlas sheets).
Angle name — source angle for captured frames, mirror target name for mirrors.
Animation clip name (e.g., "idle", "attack_light").
Frame number within the animation (0-based).
Position and size in the atlas image.
Content bounds within the frame if TrimTransparent was enabled. Null otherwise.
Pivot point normalized to frame dimensions (0,0 = top-left; default: 0.5, 0.85).
Frame display duration in seconds.
True if this frame is a horizontal/vertical flip of .
Source frame index when is true. Null for captured frames.

member.summary: Per-frame metadata within a sprite sheet. Describes a single frame's position in an atlas, its animation context, pivot point, and whether it is a mirror of another frame.

remarks.para: Captured frames come first (indices 0 through N-1), mirror frames are appended after (indices N through N+M-1). This ordering is deterministic and stable.
Mirror frames share their source frame's atlas — no duplicate pixels exist in the atlas. The game engine applies a horizontal or vertical flip at render time using the flag and .

member.param: Global frame index (0-based, unique across all frames in the sprite sheet).
Which atlas image this frame resides in (0 for single-atlas sheets).
Angle name — source angle for captured frames, mirror target name for mirrors.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.Metadata.SpriteSheet
member.summary: Complete output metadata for a sprite capture session. Contains the variant that was captured, the camera rig used, atlas image info, all animations with per-angle frame maps, and all frames (both captured and mirror entries). Serializes to a canonical JSON format via SpriteSheetSerializer.

remarks.para: The list contains captured frames first (indices 0 through N-1), followed by mirror frames (indices N through N+M-1). This ordering is deterministic.

is optional game-specific metadata embedded in the output JSON. It is not read, validated, or interpreted by any SDK component. Example uses: game build version, character class tag, content pipeline batch ID.

member.param: Schema version (e.g., "1.0").
Generator identifier (e.g., "BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteComposer").
Timestamp when the capture was performed.
The character variant that was captured (model, equipment, materials, scale).
Camera rig used for capture (source angles only, no mirror targets).
Atlas image descriptors (one or more if frames overflowed a single atlas).
All captured animations with per-angle frame index maps.
All frames — captured frames first, then mirror frames appended.
Optional game-specific opaque metadata. Null if not provided.

member.summary: Flips a pivot point along the specified axis. Horizontal: X becomes 1-X. Vertical: Y becomes 1-Y.

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.DepthToNormal
member.summary: Converts a depth buffer to a tangent-space normal map using Sobel 3x3 convolution.

remarks.para: The Sobel operator estimates horizontal and vertical gradients of the depth field at each pixel. These gradients are used to construct a surface normal vector pointing outward from the sprite plane (positive Z in tangent space). The resulting normal map encodes each component from [-1, 1] to [0, 255].
Boundary pixels use clamped sampling — edge values are repeated rather than wrapped or zeroed, preventing dark-border artifacts on normal maps.
Reference: Irwin Sobel and Gary Feldman, "A 3x3 Isotropic Gradient Operator for Image Processing" (Stanford AI Project, 1968).

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.DepthToNormal.Generate(System.Single[],System.Int32,System.Int32,BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.NormalMapOptions)
member.summary: Generates a tangent-space normal map from a depth buffer using Sobel 3x3 convolution.
member.param: Depth values in the range 0.0 (near) to 1.0 (far). Must contain exactly * elements.
Width of the depth buffer in pixels.
Height of the depth buffer in pixels.
Normal map generation options (strength, blur radius).
member.returns: An RGBA byte array of size * * 4, containing the tangent-space normal map with alpha set to 255 for all pixels.
member.exception: Thrown when length does not equal * .

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.DepthToNormal.S(System.Single[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Samples the depth buffer at (x, y) with clamped boundary handling. Edge values are repeated for out-of-bounds coordinates.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.DepthToNormal.GaussianBlur(System.Single[],System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Applies a separable Gaussian blur to the depth buffer. The kernel is computed from the given radius using a standard Gaussian distribution.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.DepthToNormal.BuildGaussianKernel(System.Int32)
member.summary: Builds a normalized 1D Gaussian kernel for the given radius. Kernel size is (2 * radius + 1). Sigma is radius / 3.0 (covers ~99.7% of distribution).

member.name: T:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.NormalMapOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for depth-to-normal-map conversion via . Controls normal map intensity and optional pre-blur smoothing of the depth buffer.
member.param: Normal map intensity multiplier. Higher values produce more pronounced normals.
Gaussian blur radius applied to the depth buffer before Sobel convolution. 0 disables blur.

member.name: M:BeyondImmersion.Bannou.SpriteTheory.NormalMap.NormalMapOptions.#ctor(System.Single,System.Int32)
